item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this, it introduced me to La Rue and looks like I will need to trudge into Sarkic things that honestly give me the heeby-jeebies, body horror being a trigger for me.

More to read, more to absorb! This stood well for me, and Legba's involvement and portrayal were fantastic. I'm interested to see how deeply into their lore these will dive and how others will be portrayed in the Foundation's universe.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I was involved in this. I’m pretty proud of that. However, I mostly just provided insight on some of my characters for MalyceGraves, wrote the description, and they worked1 their black magics from there. My thanks, therefore, go to them! In Memoria, Adytum is an awesome project, and I look forward to both its completion and the part I might play in it!

Footnotes
1. Wrote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And that's totally fair! What I was asking fundamentally changed the nature of the piece. Here, you are telling the story that you want to tell, and that should absolutely be commended! I really don't want to imply this piece is bad! It isn't! I, Riemann, personally feel that it's more about expanding existing lore than telling its own self contained story. And that's really just my opinion.

+ At risk of furthering the comments
- :p

But a small point - I think the strength of 173 was just how much it implied. And that's not really a hot take :p But when 173 popped up, it was so weird and specific that people could infer so much stuff. It was like the Mos Eisley Cantina scene - it's got confidence. There's a whole universe, and everything has a story and explanation, but we're here getting a small slice. We don't need any more explanation because the creator had so much confidence in the story they were telling that they didn't feel the need to explain what space jazz is1.

I think this piece relies and expands, rather than implies. And as other comments have pointed out, it does a great job of that. People are excited for what's about to go down.

Footnotes
1. George is really bad at naming things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to chime in here and say something.

When Riemann and I discussed this during critique, he raised all these very valid points. I considered them and had a few lengthy discussions with other critiquers afterwards.

+ Arguments
– hide block

In the end I made the conscious choice to disregard several of these points, not because I didn't feel like they were legitimate, but that they simply didn't outweigh my opinions on how this piece stands. There are several things that we accept exist in a kind of "universal headcanon". The Foundation is a giant bureaucratic entity that exists to protect normalcy. The Foundation is a global entity and for the most part tries to act solely to protect normalcy, no matter the cost. The Foundation generally has the technology/financial capacity/manpower/facilities necessary to execute the special containment procedures listed within the first part of the document.

Now, each of these don't need to be explained in order for the piece to stand alone. I think that we would RAPIDLY lose readership if we included banal things like financial statements or a cost/benefit analysis for every single SCP as a part of the standard.

Since I am very much including this within the context of a pre-existing canon, similar understood things must apply. Some of my favorite authors and stories exist within the context of "dropping" the reader immediately into the pre-existing lives of the characters within, then allowing contextual clues provided along the way to showcase the backstory.

I have tried to encapsulate that idea here. Yes, there is pre-existing architecture here for the reader to look back on, should they choose. We don't require readers to go back and check up on any of the History of the Foundation documentation in order to read and understand whatever the current piece is. Hell, when 173 was written, none of that existed.

So, I chose to allow the curiosity of the reader guide their eye. Should they read this and wish to know more, the links exist to allow that. If they do not, then they are welcome to enjoy or dislike this piece on its own merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you hit upon my issue with the piece by way of this explanation. If this is an introduction to things that haven't happened yet, why am I not reading about those things? They sound interesting. But I can't read about those things here, because this isn't actually a story, it's a chapter in a larger narrative.

The article conveys what La Rue Macabre and Darkwater Lodge are, but it relies too much on my pre-existing knowledge to make me care. Is shit about to pop off? I don't know, because I'm expected to be familiar with both GOIs to know the significance of La Rue not liking the Foundation anymore.

Again, I don't want to insinuate that this is poorly written. That's clearly not the case! But I feel that an article should act as a standalone piece, and I do not feel this does that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved this in chat, was glad I got a chance to see it before its final form.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone more in the know with the sarkic goings-on, this is really more of an introduction to things that haven't happened yet. All you really need to know going into this is that La Rue Macabre is a voodoo-inspired LoI in New Orleans, the Darkwater Lodge are sarkic cultists, and shit is about to pop off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also read this in chat and whilst I am not up to speed on the Sarkic stuff by any means, I really got the impression that it was written by someone who is. No infodumping or anything like that, which is great - in-universe, whoever's reading this would most likely be well-versed in the Sarkic activities. I got the gist of the article well enough and the dialogue was an enjoyable read, so quality-wise I give it a +1. Will definitely have to check out the hubs relating to this for more context, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in chat. As was pointed out there, this is very well written - but as a neophyte to the 5+ hubs that this references, it doesn't stand under its own weight to me. There's a lot of stuff happening but it is almost entirely references to other works. The standalone part of this article could probably be summed up in two or three sentences. Everything else exists to move the chains on other articles and stories.

Don't get me wrong - I'm sure this is an amazing piece of worldbuilding in its own right. But without catching up on all those hubs, it isn't for me.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
“Never think that war, no matter how necessary, nor how justified, is not a crime.”
— Ernest Hemingway, 1946

And so the ball really starts rolling in Memoria, Adytum. May the gods have mercy on us all.

Thank you to my critters. Y'all the real MVPs.
